notes: Plan skill shows a smaller delta (+50%) than explore/define/architect. The baseline already produces decent decompositions — the main gaps are structural (no AFK/HITL, no mermaid, no verification commands, no line numbers).

The skill's biggest weakness: waivers with-skill still fell into horizontal layering for issues 01-03, and file counts exceeded the 1-6 target. This is the only skill that didn't achieve 100% in at least one eval.
Vertical slice decomposition is the hardest assertion — both configs struggle with it, though the skill does better (1/2 pass vs 0/2).
Assertions that pass in both configs: input classification (100%), file count (75%+ both), dependency ordering (100%). These don't differentiate.
Strongest differentiators: AFK/HITL classification (100% vs 0%), overview format (100% vs 0%), line numbers (100% vs 0%), issue template completeness (100% vs 0%).
Grader notes: baseline issues contain detailed TS signatures and SQL queries despite failing structural assertions — the substance is there, the format isn't.
Time/token difference is smallest of all skills (~23% more time, ~54% more tokens). Plan skill's overhead is mainly in deep codebase reading for line numbers.
